seoTitle: How to Delete a User in the Google Workspace Console
metaDescription: Follow this quick guide to deleting a user in the Google Workspace Admin Console, from locating the account to confirming the deletion is complete.
intro: Removing a user from Google Workspace is a routine admin task when staff leave or when you're cleaning up unused accounts. This guide walks through navigating to the Admin Console, locating the specific user, and safely deleting their account. It's aimed at Workspace admins who want a quick, straightforward reference for user management.

steps.heading: Navigate to the Google Workspace Admin Console
steps.body: Log in as an admin and open the Admin Console home page, where user management options are available.

steps.heading: Locate the user you want to remove
steps.body: Search or browse the Users list to find the specific account you need to delete.

steps.heading: Delete the user from Workspace
steps.body: Select the user and choose the delete option, confirming the action to remove them from your organization.

steps.heading: Verify the deletion
steps.body: Check the Users list again to confirm the account no longer appears, ensuring the removal was successful.

faq.question: What happens to a deleted user's data in Google Workspace?
faq.answer: Deleting a user can affect access to their files and email, so admins should review data transfer or backup options before finalizing the deletion.

faq.question: Can I undo a user deletion in Google Workspace?
faq.answer: Google Workspace typically allows account restoration within a limited window after deletion, but it's not guaranteed indefinitely, so act carefully.

faq.question: Do I need admin permissions to delete a Google Workspace user?
faq.answer: Yes, only users with the appropriate admin role in the Admin Console can delete accounts.

faq.question: Will deleting a user free up a license for someone else?
faq.answer: Yes, removing a user typically frees up their assigned Workspace license for reassignment to another account.
